subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Vulnerable Populations in Heat
description: One-minute skill: Vulnerable Populations in Heat.

hook.text: The 2003 European heat wave killed 70,000 people. Most were elderly, alone, in un-airconditioned homes.

buildup.text: Elderly people have reduced sweat output and thirst sensation—they overheat without feeling it.

discovery.text: Certain medications—diuretics, beta-blockers, anticholinergics—impair the body's heat response.

twist.text: Social isolation kills in heat waves: those who live alone have no one to notice their symptoms.

climax.text: Neighborhood check-in programs during heat waves save lives by ensuring the vulnerable are reached.

punchline.text: Heat kills the old, the sick, and the alone. A neighbor's knock can save a life.

quiz.question: Why are elderly people especially vulnerable during heat waves?
options: Reduced sweat output and diminished thirst sensation
